trivia_bullets.text: Battle for Zendikar (set code BFZ) released October 2, 2015 — the second visit to the plane of Zendikar, framed as an all-out war between Zendikar's survivors and the Eldrazi titans Ulamog and Kozilek, who had been freed from the Hedron network [Scryfall: set BFZ].

Molten Nursery is one of three BFZ cards that trigger on colorless spell casts — the others being Kozilek's Sentinel (a creature that gets +1/+0) and Nettle Drone (a creature that untaps and pings each opponent). Together they formed a coherent 'devoid payoff' package at common/uncommon for BFZ Limited [Scryfall: BFZ set search].

The card carries the devoid frame treatment — a visual design decision by Wizards R&D to distinguish colorless Eldrazi-affiliated cards from normally-colored cards in BFZ. Despite being cast with red mana, Molten Nursery has no color (colors: [] in Scryfall data) and thus triggers its own ability [Scryfall].

"Some detours just aren't worth it." — Raff Slugeater, goblin shortcutter [Scryfall]. The speaker is an unnamed goblin; the quote frames the volcanic Eldrazi environment as an obstacle to be avoided, adding a sardonic street-level perspective on the Eldrazi invasion.

Legal in Pioneer, Modern, Legacy, and Commander. EDHREC rank 21876 — a low-inclusion fringe card; its single-damage output requires a dedicated colorless-spell gameplan to generate meaningful value, and the 3-mana cost is steep for such a conditional enchantment [Scryfall: edhrec_rank field].
